<!-- Code generated by gen-dns-providers; DO NOT EDIT. -->

Configuration for the [Tencent EdgeOne](https://edgeone.ai) DNS provider.

See the [lego documentation](https://go-acme.github.io/lego/dns/edgeone/) for full details.

```yaml
letsEncrypt:
  dnsProvider: "edgeone"
  dnsCredentials:
    # EDGEONE_SECRET_ID: Access key ID
    secretID: ""
    # EDGEONE_SECRET_KEY: Access Key secret
    secretKey: ""
    # EDGEONE_HTTP_TIMEOUT: API request timeout in seconds (Default: 30)
    httpTimeout: ""
    # EDGEONE_POLLING_INTERVAL: Time between DNS propagation check in seconds (Default: 30)
    pollingInterval: ""
    # EDGEONE_PROPAGATION_TIMEOUT: Maximum waiting time for DNS propagation in seconds (Default: 1200)
    propagationTimeout: ""
    # EDGEONE_REGION: Region
    region: ""
    # EDGEONE_SESSION_TOKEN: Access Key token
    sessionToken: ""
    # EDGEONE_TTL: The TTL of the TXT record used for the DNS challenge in seconds (Default: 60)
    ttl: ""
    # EDGEONE_ZONES_MAPPING: Mapping between DNS zones and site IDs. (ex: 'example.org:id1,example.com:id2')
    zonesMapping: ""
```

You may also use the raw lego environment-variable names as keys instead of the normalized names.
Credentials may also be supplied directly as system environment variables, in which case `dnsCredentials` can be omitted.
